subject: exfat: cleanup from kernel version conditions.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: The fork's sdfat-lineage exfat driver becomes less maintainable; older kernel compatibility shims are retained. However, this driver is entirely absent from 6.18 build — current build uses vanilla exfat from mainline.

notes: GROUPED COMMIT — part of patch-provenance.md §3.8 Class G row grouping all six exfat-replacement commits (8b6b8c2f5, df35bdb27, 858322ce6, 5220d6686, 7f7148c1f, 99a2c80d0) under one row with subject 'Remove mainline exFAT; add out-of-tree exFAT with symlink support; cleanups'. Patch-provenance guidance (§1 Pilot Lessons) explicitly requires analyzing ONLY the assigned commit; grouping is the failure mode this project exists to fix. Per ADR 0010 (accepted 2026-07-12), the entire sdfat-lineage exfat driver was dropped in favor of vanilla exfat from mainline. ADR 0019 (accepted 2026-07-14) amended ADR 0010 to restore symlink support via carried patch 0031 (0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch) against mainline exfat. Current 6.18 build uses vanilla fs/exfat/ (CONFIG_EXFAT_FS=y), not the fork's fs/exfat/ sdfat lineage. This commit is a cleanup within the dropped driver and has zero effect on current build. Escalation required due to grouped-row provenance entry. sonnet-verified: disposition=dropped-obsolete and severity=none confirmed correct — equivalence=null is appropriately left unset (no upstream comparison asserted, consistent with sibling exfat-group records after correction).
